column1: WPAs serve as protective buffers around peatland core areas to minimize hydrologic and ecological impacts from adjacent land uses. This layer supports planning
column4: and peatland protection work across the Department of Natural Resources. It should not be used for regulatory purposes.

column1: • The Minnesota Peatland Protection Act (Minn. Stat. 84.035-84.036) lists Peatland SNAs by county but does not include legal descriptions or reference WPAs.
• WPAs are recognized in Minn. R. 6132.2000
column3: which restricts mining within WPAs and incorporates the 1984 peatland report by reference as the authoritative source for WPAs. This report also explicitly states that peat mining is prohibited within WPAs.

column1: • The Minnesota State Law Library holds a DNR volume titled "Legal description and maps of peatland protection management areas" (Dec. 3
column3: which appears to contain the legal descriptions referenced (but not included) in the published 1984 report.

column1: This dataset represents the Peatland Watershed Protection Areas (WPAs) associated with Minnesota's 18 Peatland Scientific and Natural Areas (SNAs). WPAs were delineated in the early-mid 1980s as part of the Protection of Ecologically Significant Peatlands in Minnesota project and are referenced in Minnesota Rules 6132.2000 as areas where mining is restricted. The boundaries in this shapefile were likely digitized in 2007 from the original hand drawn maps contained in the 1984 peatland report.Metadata PDFDownloadData DownloadDownloadGeopackageDownload
• Not suitable for parcel level regulatory determinations.
• Consult the 1984 report and legal descriptions for any questions surrounding original proposed and current boundaries appearing in the shapefile (2007).
• Users should be aware of the historical and legislative constraints that shaped these boundaries.
This file is a human and machine readable equivalent of the layer
exported from the ESRI personal geodatabase
and was generated to back up and archive the parent dataset
for posterity in a non-proprietary text format.
Row field values are separated by a pipe character |to avoid confusion with commas in WKT geometry.
